html:
[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 玉清三宝 - Refers to the Three Treasures of the Jade Purity, likely a reference to Daoist or Buddhist sacred objects or concepts.
  • 举进士第 - Means 'passed the imperial examination for the jinshi degree,' indicating the highest level of academic achievement in the imperial examination system.
  • 寓游于蜀 - Means 'to live and travel in Shu' (the region around modern-day Sichuan). The term '寓游' suggests a temporary residence or travel.
  • 胜地 - Refers to scenic or beautiful places.
  • 花酒宴 - A euphemism for a banquet or feast, often associated with drinking and entertainment.
  • 虽夜不殆 - Means 'though it goes late into the night,' indicating that the activities continue without tiring.
  • 请者曰 - Means 'someone invited them, saying,' introducing a request or invitation.
  • 郡南去十里有郑氏亭 - Means 'ten miles south of the city, there is a Zheng's Pavilion.' The term '郑氏亭' likely refers to a pavilion or garden owned by the Zheng family.
  • 真尘外境也 - Means 'truly a place beyond the mundane world,' suggesting a serene or idyllic setting.
  • 愿偕去 - Means 'I wish to go with you,' expressing a desire to accompany the group.
  • 喜甚 - Means 'very happy,' indicating a strong emotional response.
  • 遂与俱 - Means 'so he went with them,' concluding the invitation and the decision to join.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杜陵韦弇，字景昭 - Refers to Wei Yan (韦弇), a person from Duling (杜陵), a place in ancient China. The name '景昭' is his courtesy name.
  • 开元中 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang during the Kaiyuan period (713-741 AD), a time of cultural and economic prosperity.
  • 进士第 - Refers to the highest degree in the imperial examination system, indicating that Wei Yan achieved a high level of scholarly success.
  • 蜀 - Refers to the region around modern-day Sichuan, known for its scenic beauty and cultural richness.
  • 郑氏亭 - Likely refers to a specific pavilion or garden owned by the Zheng family, which was a common feature in classical Chinese literature as a symbol of leisure and elegance.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '寓游于蜀' is straightforward but can be read as a continuous action, emphasizing the temporary nature of Wei Yan's stay in Shu.
  • The sequence '花酒宴，虽夜不殆' describes a series of activities that continue late into the night, with '虽夜不殆' serving as a modifier for the entire preceding clause.
  • The long descriptive phrase '郡南去十里有郑氏亭，亭起苑中，真尘外境也' must be read as a single unit, with '亭起苑中' providing additional detail about the location of the pavilion.
  • The clause '愿偕去' is straightforward but can be read as a direct request or invitation, emphasizing the speaker's desire to join the group.

Proper Nouns

  • 杜陵 - A place name, referring to a region in ancient China.
  • 韦弇 - The name of the person being described.
  • 景昭 - The courtesy name of Wei Yan.
  • 开元 - The era name of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 蜀 - The name of the region where the events take place.
  • 郑氏亭 - The name of the pavilion or garden.

[205]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尘外境 - A literary expression meaning a place beyond the mundane world, a paradise or celestial abode.
  • 刘、阮事 - Refers to the legendary story of Liu and Ruan, who were invited to a celestial realm by fairies.
  • 真所谓尘外境也 - Emphasizes that the place is truly beyond the ordinary, a realm of extraordinary beauty.
  • 凝立若伫，半掉云袂，飘飘然 - Describes the gods as standing still with their sleeves fluttering like clouds, creating an ethereal and mystical atmosphere.
  • 纹绣杳渺，殆不可识 - Describes the intricate patterns and decorations as faint and almost indistinguishable, suggesting a mysterious and otherworldly quality.
  • 不意今日得为刘、阮，幸何甚哉 - The narrator expresses surprise and happiness at being treated like Liu and Ruan, as if invited to a celestial realm.
  • 是以假郑氏之亭以命君，果副吾志 - The gods explain that they used the Zheng family's pavilion to invite the narrator, and he has indeed fulfilled their wish.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 郑氏亭 - A pavilion belonging to the Zheng family, likely a place of significance in the local area.
  • 玉清 - A Taoist concept referring to the highest level of purity and clarity, often associated with the Jade Emperor or other high-ranking deities.
  • 玉清宫 - The Jade Clarity Palace, a sacred place in Taoist mythology.
  • 下第进士 - A scholar who has failed the imperial examination, often used to describe a person of high moral integrity but not yet recognized by the state.

Proper Nouns

  • 郑氏亭 - The Zheng family's pavilion.
  • 尘外境 - A place beyond the mundane world.
  • 刘、阮 - The legendary scholars Liu and Ruan.
  • 玉清 - The highest level of purity and clarity in Taoism.
  • 玉清宫 - The Jade Clarity Palace.
  • 下第进士 - A scholar who has failed the imperial examination.

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 雖然，此仙府也 - Concessive phrase meaning 'Although this is a fairy realm.'
  • 雖云不可滞世间人 - Means 'Although it is said that mortals cannot linger here.'
  • 君居之，固无损耳 - Means 'You can live here without any harm.'
  • 幸不以为疑 - Means 'Please do not doubt it.'
  • 即命酒乐宴亭中 - Means 'Immediately, they ordered a feast and music in the pavilion.'
  • 丝竹尽举 - Means 'All the string and wind instruments were played.'
  • 飘然泠然，凌玄越冥 - Means 'Floating and cool, transcending the mysterious and the profound.'
  • 不为人间声曲 - Means 'It was not of earthly tunes.'
  • 酒既酣 - Means 'When the wine had been drunk deeply.'
  • 吾闻唐天子尚神仙 - Means 'I have heard that the Tang emperor reveres immortals.'
  • 吾有新乐一曲，曰《紫云》 - Means 'I have a new piece of music, called 'Purple Cloud.''
  • 愿授圣主 - Means 'I wish to present it to the Holy Emperor.'
  • 君，唐人也 - Means 'You, being a Tang person.'
  • 为吾传之一进 - Means 'to convey it to the emperor once.'
  • 可乎 - Means 'Can you do this?'
  • 弇，一儒也 - Means 'I, a scholar.'
  • 在长安中，徒为区区于尘土间 - Means 'In Chang'an, I am merely preoccupied with trivial matters.'
  • 望天子门且不可见之 - Means 'I cannot even see the gates of the emperor's palace.'
  • 又非知音者 - Means 'Nor is there anyone who understands music.'
  • 曷能致是 - Means 'How can I bring this about?'
  • 君既不能，吾将以梦传于天子可也 - Means 'Since you cannot, I will convey it to the emperor through a dream.'
  • 吾有三宝，将以赠君 - Means 'I have three treasures, which I will give to you.'
  • 能使君富敌王侯 - Means 'These can make you as wealthy as kings and dukes.'
  • 君其受之 - Means 'Please accept them.'
  • 乃命左右取其宝 - Means 'He then ordered his attendants to bring the treasures.'
  • 始出一杯，其色碧，而光莹洞澈 - Means 'First, he brought out a cup, green in color, and brilliantly transparent.'
  • 顾谓弇曰：“碧瑶杯也。” - Means 'He looked at me and said, 'This is a green jade cup.''
  • 又出一枕，似玉，微红 - Means 'Then he brought out a pillow, resembling jade, slightly red.'
  • 曰：“红蕤枕也。” - Means 'He said, 'This is a red phoenix pillow.''
  • 又出一小函，其色紫，亦似玉，而莹澈则过之 - Means 'Then he brought out a small box, purple in color, also resembling jade, but even more transparent.'
  • 曰：“紫玉函也。” - Means 'He said, 'This is a purple jade box.''
  • 已而皆授弇 - Means 'Afterward, he gave all of them to me.'
  • 弇拜谢别去 - Means 'I bowed in gratitude and left.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 仙府 - Refers to a fairy realm or仙境, a place where immortals reside.
  • 唐天子 - Refers to the emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 长安 - The capital city of the Tang Dynasty, now known as Xi'an.
  • 碧瑶杯 - A green jade cup, symbolizing purity and elegance.
  • 红蕤枕 - A red phoenix pillow, symbolizing nobility and luxury.
  • 紫玉函 - A purple jade box, symbolizing wealth and power.

Proper Nouns

  • 唐天子 - The emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 长安 - The capital city of the Tang Dynasty, now known as Xi'an.
  • 碧瑶杯 - A green jade cup.
  • 红蕤枕 - A red phoenix pillow.
  • 紫玉函 - A purple jade box.

[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 行未及一里 - Literally 'did not walk a mile,' used to indicate a short distance.
  • 回望其亭，茫然无有 - 'Looking back at the pavilion, it was nowhere to be seen,' conveying a sense of sudden disappearance or illusion.
  • 弇异之 - 'Yan was astonished by it,' where '异' means to be surprised or astonished.
  • 亦竟不知何所也 - 'He still did not know where it was,' indicating uncertainty or confusion.
  • 遂挈其宝还长安 - 'So he took the treasure back to Chang'an,' where '挈' means to carry or take.
  • 明年下第 - 'The following year, he failed the examination,' where '下第' refers to failing the imperial examination.
  • 东游至广陵 - 'Traveled east to Guangling,' where '东游' means to travel east.
  • 因以其宝集于广陵市 - 'So he gathered his treasure in the Guangling market,' where '集' means to gather or collect.
  • 有胡人见而拜曰 - 'A foreigner saw it and bowed, saying,' where '胡人' refers to a foreigner, often from Central Asia.
  • 此天下之奇宝也 - 'This is a rare treasure in the world,' where '奇宝' means a rare or extraordinary treasure.
  • 虽千万年，人无得者 - 'Even after a thousand years, no one has obtained it,' emphasizing the rarity of the treasure.
  • 君何得而有 - 'How did you obtain it?' where '何得而有' is a rhetorical question asking about the origin of possession.
  • 乃玉清真三宝也 - 'It is the Three Treasures of the Jade Clear Truth,' where '玉清真三宝' refers to a specific set of treasures.
  • 遂以数千万为直而易之 - 'So he exchanged it for several million in value,' where '为直' means to set the value or price.
  • 弇由是建甲第 - 'Yan then built a grand mansion,' where '建甲第' means to build a grand or prestigious residence.
  • 居广陵中为豪士 - 'Lived in Guangling as a wealthy and influential person,' where '豪士' means a wealthy and influential individual.
  • 竟卒于白衣也 - 'Ultimately died as a commoner,' where '白衣' refers to an ordinary person without official rank.
  • 知 - A particle used to indicate the end of a section or a pause, often omitted in translation.
  • ○三宝村 - 'Three Treasures Village,' where '○' is a formatting mark used in classical texts.
  • 扶风县之西南 - 'In the southwest of Fufeng County,' where '扶风县' refers to a specific county.
  • 胡老相传云 - 'An old man from the Hu people said,' where '胡老' refers to an elderly foreigner.
  • 建村之时 - 'When the village was founded,' where '建村' means to establish or found a village.
  • 有宝气 - 'There was a treasure aura,' where '宝气' refers to a mystical or auspicious presence.
  • 启发将自有时耳 - 'The time for its revelation will come,' where '启发' means revelation or enlightenment.
  • 此何宝乎 - 'What kind of treasure is this?' where '何宝乎' is a question asking about the nature of the treasure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 长安 - The ancient capital of China, now modern-day Xi'an.
  • 广陵 - The ancient name for Yangzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 胡人 - Refers to foreigners, often from Central Asia, who were known for their trade and cultural exchanges with China.
  • 玉清真三宝 - A specific set of treasures associated with Daoist or mystical traditions, though the exact nature of these treasures is not specified in the text.
  • 扶风县 - A county in Shaanxi Province, historically significant for its proximity to the ancient capital of Chang'an.
  • 三宝村 - A village named after the Three Treasures, possibly a place of historical or cultural significance.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 行未及一里，回望其亭，茫然无有 - This sequence of actions and observations must be read as a continuous narrative, where Yan travels a short distance and then notices the sudden disappearance of the pavilion.
  • 弇异之，亦竟不知何所也 - The sentence structure is elliptical, but it conveys that Yan was astonished and still did not know where the pavilion was.
  • 遂挈其宝还长安 - The sentence is straightforward, but it implies that Yan took the treasure back to Chang'an after the mysterious event.
  • 明年下第，东游至广陵 - This sequence of events must be read as a chronological progression, where Yan failed the examination and then traveled east to Guangling.
  • 因以其宝集于广陵市 - The sentence is elliptical, but it means that Yan gathered his treasure in the Guangling market.
  • 有胡人见而拜曰 - The sentence introduces a foreigner who sees the treasure and bows, indicating respect or awe.
  • 此天下之奇宝也 - The sentence is a direct statement about the treasure's rarity.
  • 虽千万年，人无得者 - The sentence emphasizes the rarity of the treasure.
  • 君何得而有 - The sentence is a rhetorical question asking about the origin of possession.
  • 乃玉清真三宝也 - The sentence identifies the treasure as the Three Treasures of the Jade Clear Truth.
  • 遂以数千万为直而易之 - The sentence describes the exchange of the treasure for a large sum of money.
  • 弇由是建甲第 - The sentence indicates that Yan built a grand mansion after the exchange.
  • 居广陵中为豪士 - The sentence describes Yan's status as a wealthy and influential person in Guangling.
  • 竟卒于白衣也 - The sentence concludes with Yan's death as a commoner, suggesting a decline in his status.
  • 扶风县之西南，有三宝村 - The sentence introduces the location of the Three Treasures Village.
  • 胡老相传云 - The sentence introduces an old man from the Hu people who tells a story.
  • 建村之时，有胡僧谓村人曰 - The sentence describes the founding of the village and the words of a foreign monk.
  • 此地有宝气，而今莫得之，其启发将自有时耳 - The sentence explains the monk's prediction about the treasure.
  • 是何宝乎 - The sentence is a question asking about the nature of the treasure.
  • 乃玉清真三宝也 - The sentence identifies the treasure as the Three Treasures of the Jade Clear Truth.
  • 因以“三宝”名其村，盖识其事 - The sentence explains why the village was named 'Three Treasures.'

Proper Nouns

  • 长安 - The ancient capital of China, now modern-day Xi'an.
  • 广陵 - The ancient name for Yangzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 胡人 - Refers to foreigners, often from Central Asia.
  • 玉清真三宝 - A specific set of treasures associated with Daoist or mystical traditions.
  • 扶风县 - A county in Shaanxi Province.
  • 三宝村 - A village named after the Three Treasures.

[208]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 开成元年春 - Specific date in the Chinese calendar, referring to the spring of the first year of the Kai Cheng era during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 黑簪帻 - A formal headgear worn by ancient Chinese officials, indicating the person's status.
  • 被广袂之衣 - A type of clothing with wide sleeves, suggesting the person's attire.
  • 腰佩长剑 - Wearing a long sword at the waist, indicating the person's martial status or authority.
  • 仪状峻古 - A description of the person's dignified and ancient appearance.
  • 吾尝仕东汉，当光武时 - Refers to serving in the Eastern Han Dynasty during the reign of Emperor Guangwu.
  • 与飞将马公同征交趾 - Indicates serving alongside the Flying General Ma Gong in a campaign against Jiaozhi (modern-day Vietnam).
  • 尝得南人之宝 - Refers to obtaining treasures from the southern people.
  • 其后马公遭谤，以为多掠南货，尽载以归 - Describes how Ma Gong was later slandered for allegedly looting goods from the south and bringing them back.
  • 光武怒，将命索其家 - Indicates Emperor Guangwu's anger and his order to search Ma Gong's home.
  • 吾惧且及祸，故埋于此地 - Expresses the fear of being implicated in the scandal and the decision to bury the treasures to avoid trouble.
  • 言未讫而寤 - Means that the person finished speaking and then woke up.
  • 村中民夜梦 - Describes a villager having a dream at night.
  • 邻伍中 - Refers to the village or neighborhood.
  • 仲夏夕 - Refers to the evening of the middle summer (July).
  • 云月阴晦 - Describes a cloudy and dim night.
  • 炯然有光 - Indicates a bright light.
  • 若曳练焉 - Compares the light to a long piece of silk being dragged.
  • 久而不灭 - Means the light remained for a long time without disappearing.
  • 牧竖惊，告其父 - Describes a young herdsman being surprised and telling his father.
  • 驰往视之 - Means to rush to see it.
  • 植榫以表之 - Indicates planting a stake to mark the spot.
  • 携锸具 - Refers to bringing shovels and tools.
  • 穷表之下 - Means to dig down to the marked spot.
  • 深约丈余 - Indicates digging to a depth of about one zhang (approximately 3.3 meters).
  • 得一金龟 - Refers to finding a golden turtle.
  • 长二寸许 - Indicates the turtle is about two cun (approximately 6.7 cm) long.
  • 制度奇妙 - Describes the turtle's design as intricate and unusual.
  • 代所未识 - Means that such a design was not recognized in previous times.
  • 又得宝剑一 - Refers to finding another treasure sword.
  • 长二尺有四寸 - Indicates the sword is about 2.4 chi (approximately 73.2 cm) long.
  • 又得古镜一 - Refers to finding an ancient mirror.
  • 径一尺余 - Indicates the mirror has a diameter of more than one chi (approximately 33 cm).
  • 尘迹蒙然 - Describes the items being covered in dust.
  • 里人得之，遂持以诣县 - Means the villagers took the items to the county office.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 开成元年 - The first year of the Kai Cheng era (836 AD) in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 东汉 - The Eastern Han Dynasty (25-220 AD).
  • 光武 - Emperor Guangwu (Liu Xiu), the founder of the Eastern Han Dynasty.
  • 飞将马公 - The Flying General Ma Gong, a famous general during the Eastern Han Dynasty.
  • 交趾 - The ancient name for the region of Jiaozhi, which is modern-day Vietnam.
  • 南人 - Refers to people from the southern regions of China.
  • 金龟 - A golden turtle, often associated with wealth and good fortune in Chinese culture.
  • 宝剑 - A treasure sword, symbolizing power and prestige.
  • 古镜 - An ancient mirror, often valued for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 县 - The county office, a local administrative unit in ancient China.

Proper Nouns

  • 开成元年 - The first year of the Kai Cheng era (836 AD) in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 东汉 - The Eastern Han Dynasty (25-220 AD).
  • 光武 - Emperor Guangwu (Liu Xiu), the founder of the Eastern Han Dynasty.
  • 飞将马公 - The Flying General Ma Gong, a famous general during the Eastern Han Dynasty.
  • 交趾 - The ancient name for the region of Jiaozhi, which is modern-day Vietnam.
  • 南人 - Refers to people from the southern regions of China.
  • 县 - The county office, a local administrative unit in ancient China.

[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 澹然若水波之色 - Describes the sword as having a color as calm as water waves.
  • 真天下之奇宝也 - Concludes that these are truly rare treasures of the world.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 县令 - Refers to the county magistrate, a local official in ancient China.
  • 沛国 - A state during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 刘随 - The name of the county magistrate.
  • 天球、和璧 - Refers to the Heavenly Ball and the Jade Bi, which are highly valued ancient artifacts.
  • 上庠 - Refers to a high-level educational institution.
  • 岐阳帅 - The commander of Qiyang, a military position.
  • 陈君 - Refers to Chen, another official or military governor.
  • 节度岐陇 - Refers to the position of military governor of Qilong.
  • 安南 - Refers to a region in southern China, often under the jurisdiction of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 玉龙膏 - A medicinal substance with special properties.
  • 大和 - A period of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 韩约 - The name of the protector of An Nan.
  • 执金吾 - A high-ranking military and police official in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 京师乱 - Refers to the capital city being in turmoil.
  • 郑注 - A historical figure who was involved in political intrigues.

Proper Nouns

  • 县令 - County magistrate.
  • 沛国 - Pei State.
  • 刘随 - Liu Sui.
  • 天球 - Heavenly Ball.
  • 和璧 - Jade Bi.
  • 上庠 - Upper hall or high-level educational institution.
  • 岐阳帅 - Commander of Qiyang.
  • 陈君 - Chen.
  • 节度岐陇 - Military governor of Qilong.
  • 安南 - An Nan (a region in southern China).
  • 玉龙膏 - Jade Dragon Ointment.
  • 大和 - Great Harmony period.
  • 韩约 - Han Yue.
  • 执金吾 - Chief of the Gold Bearer.
  • 京师 - Capital city.
  • 郑注 - Zheng Zhu.

[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 地下肉芝 - Literal term meaning 'fleshy fungus underground,' likely a metaphor for something rare or unusual.
  • 亡其名 - Means 'lost his name,' indicating that the person's name is unknown or forgotten.
  • 下第 - Refers to failing the imperial examination.
  • 焚其書 - Means 'burned his books,' indicating a symbolic act of renouncing scholarly pursuits.
  • 絕粒吸氣 - Means 'abstained from food and breathed deeply,' referring to a form of ascetic practice.
  • 屈伸支體 - Means 'bent and stretched his limbs,' describing a form of physical exercise.
  • 冀延其壽 - Means 'hoped to extend his life,' expressing the goal of the ascetic practices.
  • 勃然發怒 - Means 'suddenly became furious,' indicating a sudden change in mood.
  • 棄聲利 - Means 'abandoned fame and profit,' indicating a renunciation of worldly pursuits.
  • 隱居潭水上 - Means 'retired to live by a lake,' describing a secluded lifestyle.
  • 従道士學神仙 - Means 'followed a Taoist to learn immortality,' indicating a pursuit of spiritual transcendence.
  • 絕粒吸氣 - Repeated, emphasizing the ascetic practices.
  • 冀得長生 - Means 'hoped to achieve immortality,' expressing the ultimate goal of the ascetic practices.
  • 衰瘠如是 - Means 'became as thin and weak as this,' describing the physical decline.
  • 豈我之心哉 - Means 'is this what I intended?' expressing disappointment or frustration.
  • 學商人逐什一之利 - Means 'learned to trade for a profit of one-tenth,' indicating a shift to commercial pursuits.
  • 資用大饒 - Means 'accumulated great wealth,' describing the success in commerce.
  • 為富家 - Means 'became a wealthy family,' indicating a change in social status.
  • 治園屋發地 - Means 'while repairing his garden, he dug the ground,' describing the discovery.
  • 得物狀類人手 - Means 'found something resembling a human hand,' describing the discovery.
  • 肥而且潤 - Means 'fleshy and moist,' describing the appearance of the object.
  • 色微紅 - Means 'slightly red in color,' providing additional detail about the object.
  • 豈非禍之芽 - Means 'isn't this a sign of impending misfortune?' expressing concern or foreboding.
  • 太歲所在 - Refers to the Tai Sui, a deity in Chinese astrology associated with the year's direction.
  • 不可興土事 - Means 'cannot undertake earthworks,' indicating a superstition or taboo.
  • 脫有犯者 - Means 'if someone were to violate this,' indicating a hypothetical scenario.
  • 當有修肉出其下 - Means 'there would be a fleshy object emerging from below,' describing a potential consequence.
  • 固不祥也 - Means 'certainly an omen of bad luck,' expressing a negative interpretation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 地下肉芝 - A metaphorical term that may refer to a rare or mystical object, possibly with connotations of immortality or supernatural significance.
  • 進士 - Refers to a degree obtained by passing the imperial examinations, indicating a scholarly background.
  • 絕粒吸氣 - Refers to a form of ascetic practice common in Taoist and Buddhist traditions, aimed at achieving spiritual or physical purity.
  • 太歲 - A deity in Chinese astrology, often associated with the year's direction and believed to influence various activities, including construction.
  • 修肉 - A term that may refer to a fleshy object or a sign of bad luck, possibly with supernatural connotations.
  • 不祥 - Refers to an omen of bad luck or misfortune, indicating a negative interpretation of the discovery.

Proper Nouns

  • 蘭陵 - A place name, possibly referring to a region in China.
  • 蕭逸人 - The name of the subject, though his full name is not provided.
  • 太歲 - The name of the deity in Chinese astrology.
  • 修肉 - A term that may refer to a fleshy object or a sign of bad luck, possibly with supernatural connotations.

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 今果有，奈何 - A question meaning 'Now that it exists, what should be done?'
  • 得肉食之，或可以免 - A conditional phrase meaning 'If one gets to eat it, perhaps one can avoid [some misfortune].'
  • 味甚美，食且尽 - Describes the food as extremely delicious and nearly consumed.
  • 自是逸人听视明，力愈壮，貌愈少，发之秃者尽黰然而长矣，齿之堕者亦骈然而生矣 - Describes the hermit's improved senses, strength, appearance, hair regrowth, and tooth regrowth.
  • 默自奇异，不敢告于人 - The hermit silently wonders at the changes and dares not tell others.
  • 后有道士至邺下，逢逸人，惊曰：“先生尝得饵仙药乎何神气清晤如是。” - A Taoist is amazed and asks if the hermit has taken immortal elixir.
  • 轸其脉 - The Taoist examines the hermit's pulse.
  • 先生尝食灵芝矣 - The Taoist guesses that the hermit has eaten lingzhi (灵芝).
  • 夫灵芝状类人手，肥而且润，色微红者是也 - Describes the appearance of lingzhi mushrooms.
  • 先生之寿，可与龟鹤齐矣 - The Taoist congratulates the hermit, saying his lifespan can match that of turtles and cranes.
  • 然不宜居尘俗间，当退休山林，弃人事，神仙可致 - Advises the hermit to retire to the mountains, abandon worldly affairs, and achieve immortality.
  • 喜而従其语，遂去，竟不知所在 - The hermit is delighted and follows the advice, leaving and disappearing without a trace.
  • 东洛有故宅，其堂奥轩级甚宏特，然居者多暴死，是以空而键之且久 - Describes an old house in Donglu that has been vacant for a long time due to frequent sudden deaths.
  • 为御史分察东台，常欲贸其宅而止焉 - Describes Lu Qian's role as a censor and his desire to buy the house.
  • 此宅有怪，不可居 - A warning that the house is haunted and not suitable for living in.
  • 吾自能弭之 - Lu Qian's confident statement that he can dispel the supernatural phenomena.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 卢虔 - A historical figure, a right censor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 东洛 - A place name, referring to the eastern part of Luoyang.

  • 暴死 - Sudden death, often attributed to supernatural causes in Chinese folklore.
  • 御史 - A censor, an official responsible for monitoring and reporting on government officials.
  • 分察东台 - A specific duty of the censor, to inspect the eastern region.
  • 贸其宅 - To buy a house.
  • 此宅有怪 - A common motif in Chinese ghost stories, where a house is haunted by spirits.

Proper Nouns

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 自大理评事解县推盐使判官 - A bureaucratic title indicating the person's official position: '大理评事' (official at the Supreme Court), '解县' (a county), '推盐使' (official in charge of salt distribution), and '判官' (administrative judge).
  • 罢职退归 - Means 'retired from office and returned home'.
  • 因治园屋 - Means 'because he was renovating his garden or house'.
  • 命家仆伐一树 - Means 'ordered his servant to cut down a tree'.
  • 既伐，而有血滂溜，汪然注地，食顷而尽 - Describes an unusual event where blood flowed from the tree after it was cut down, filling the ground in a short time.
  • 宽异之，且知为怪 - Means 'Sin felt strange about this and knew it was a supernatural occurrence'.
  • 由是闭门绝人事 - Means 'thereafter he closed his doors and cut off contact with people'.
  • 至明年冬十一月 - Refers to the eleventh month of the following year, which would be December 835.
  • 郑注、李训反 - Refers to the political conspiracy involving Zheng Zhu and Li Xun, who attempted a coup against Emperor Wenzong.
  • 宽与注连，遂诛死于左禁军中 - Means 'Sin was implicated in the conspiracy and was executed in the Left Forbidden Army'.
  • 有厉泉县民吴偃 - Refers to a man named Wu Yan from the county of Liquan, who was afflicted by a supernatural illness.
  • 家于田野间 - Means 'lived in the countryside'.
  • 有一女十岁余 - Means 'had a daughter who was over ten years old'.
  • 忽遁去，莫知所往 - Means 'suddenly disappeared, and no one knew where she went'.
  • 后数余日 - Means 'several days later'.
  • 偃梦其父谓偃曰 - Means 'Wu Yan dreamed that his father told him'.
  • 汝女今在东北隅，盖木神为崇 - Means 'Your daughter is now in the northeast corner, it seems that a tree spirit has caused this'.
  • 偃惊而寤 - Means 'Wu Yan woke up in shock'.
  • 至明日，即于东北隅穷其迹 - Means 'the next day, he went to the northeast corner to investigate the traces'.
  • 果闻有呼吟之事 - Means 'he indeed heard sounds of calling and moaning'.
  • 见其女有一穴内 - Means 'he saw his daughter inside a hole'.
  • 口甚小，然其中稍宽敞 - Means 'the entrance was very small, but inside it was somewhat spacious'.
  • 傍有古槐木，盘根极大 - Means 'there was an ancient locust tree nearby with very large roots'.
  • 于是挈之而归 - Means 'he then carried her back home'.
  • 然兀若沉醉者 - Means 'but she seemed dazed as if drunk'.
  • 会有李道士至，偃请符术呵禁 - Means 'later, a Taoist named Li arrived, and Wu Yan asked him for a talisman and exorcism'.
  • 其女忽瞬而语曰 - Means 'his daughter suddenly opened her eyes and spoke'.
  • 地东北有槐木，木有神，引某自树腹空入地下穴内，故某病 - Means 'There is a locust tree in the northeast, the tree has a spirit, it led me into the hollow inside the tree to an underground hole, and that's why I am sick'.
  • 于是伐其树 - Means 'he then cut down the tree'.
  • 后数日，女病始愈 - Means 'after several days, his daughter's illness began to improve'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大和八年 - The eighth year of the Dahe era, corresponding to 834 AD.
  • 郑注、李训反 - Refers to the February 11, 835 AD incident, known as the 'Wang Shixian Incident,' where Zheng Zhu and Li Xun attempted a coup against Emperor Wenzong, leading to their execution and the deaths of many officials.
  • 厉泉县 - An ancient county name, likely referring to a specific location in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 吴偃 - A name of a commoner, indicating a lower-class individual.
  • 木神 - Refers to a tree spirit, a common concept in Chinese folklore and mythology.

  • 厉泉县 - An ancient county name.

  • 不敢动 - 'Afraid to move,' indicating the stableman's caution.

  • 吾子无以为忧 - 'You have nothing to worry about,' offering reassurance.

  • 谨受教 - 'I will heed your advice,' indicating acceptance of the warning.
  • 韩氏稚儿有字乎 - 'Does the young Han child have a name yet?' asking about the child's naming.
  • 未也 - 'Not yet,' answering the question.
  • 吾伺有字，即编于名籍，不敢忘 - 'I will wait until he has a name, then compile him in the name register, and I will not forget,' promising to keep track.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 為振武軍都将 - Served as the commander of the Zhenwu Army. '將都' is a military title, and '為' indicates the role or position.
  • 素以犷悍聞 - '素' means 'from the beginning' or 'originally'; '以...聞' is a common construction meaning 'to be known for.'
  • 臂弓腰矢 - With bow in arm and arrow at the waist. This is a compound verb phrase describing a person armed and ready for hunting.
  • 辟侍婢，不許辄近左右 - '辟' here means to dismiss or send away; '辄' is a prohibitive particle meaning 'without permission.'
  • 扃户而寝 - '扃户' is a classical term for locking the door.
  • 發怒，過杖其家人輩 - '過杖' is a compound verb meaning 'to beat with a stick, excessively.'
  • 具白于含 - '具白' is a formal expression meaning 'to fully report' or 'to inform in detail.'
  • 于隙中潜窥 - '潜窥' means to secretly look or spy.
  • 金氏召含，且令即市糜鹿 - '即市' is a compound verb meaning 'to go buy immediately.'
  • 吾所须生者耳 - '耳' is a particle indicating limitation or emphasis, often translated as 'only' or 'merely.'
  • 含益惧 - '益' means 'increasingly' or 'more.'
  • 家人辈或窃语其事 - '窃语' means to speak secretly or whisper.
  • 色甚惭 - '色' refers to facial expression or demeanor.
  • 自是竟不还 - '自是' means 'from then on'; '竟' is used here to emphasize that the wolf never came back.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太原王含 - A person from Taiyuan named Wang Han. '太原' is a place name, referring to the region of Taiyuan in northern China.
  • 振武军都将 - '振武军' is a military unit, likely from the Tang or later dynasties. '都将' is a military rank, equivalent to a general or commander.
  • 金氏 - A surname, 'Jin.' This is a common Chinese surname, and the text refers to her as '金氏,' the wife of Wang Han.
  • 胡人女 - '胡人' refers to people from the northern nomadic tribes, often used in Chinese historical texts to describe non-Han ethnic groups.
  • 善弓马 - Skilled in archery and horseback riding. This reflects the traditional martial skills of the Hu people, who were often known for their horsemanship and archery.
  • 取熊鹿狐兔 - Hunting animals such as bears, deer, foxes, and rabbits. These were common game animals in ancient China.
  • 狼 - 'Wolf.' In Chinese folklore and historical texts, wolves are often associated with supernatural or dangerous forces.
  • 糜鹿 - 'Deer or elk.' This is a specific type of animal, often used in Chinese texts as a food source or sacrificial offering.

  • 太原 - A place name, should be translated consistently as 'Taiyuan.'
  • 振武军 - A military unit, should be translated consistently as 'Zhenwu Army.'
  • 都将 - A military rank, should be translated consistently as 'commander' or 'general.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 晋阳以北，地寒而少竹 - Literal meaning 'north of Jinyang, the land is cold and has few bamboos.' Sets the geographical and environmental context.
  • 故居人多种苇成林 - 'Therefore, the residents often grow reeds into forests,' indicating a common practice due to the scarcity of bamboo.
  • 所以代南方之竹也 - 'To replace the bamboo of the south,' explaining the purpose of growing reeds.
  • 置余食于其舍 - 'Leave leftover food in their house,' describing the action of leaving food.
  • 至明日辄不知其所在 - 'By the next day, it was always gone,' indicating the mysterious disappearance of the food.
  • 其民有贮缯帛于其室者，亦亡之 - 'Some of the residents who stored silk and cloth in their rooms also lost them,' further emphasizing the mysterious nature of the disappearances.
  • 民窃异焉 - 'The residents secretly found it strange,' showing their reaction to the unexplained events.
  • 后夜闻婴儿号者甚众 - 'Later at night, they heard many babies crying,' describing the unusual sounds.
  • 迫而听之，则阒然矣 - 'When they approached to listen, it was silent,' indicating the transient nature of the sounds.
  • 明夕又闻，民惧且甚 - 'The next evening, they heard it again, and the residents were even more afraid,' showing the increasing fear.
  • 即相与芟除其林，薙其草 - 'They then worked together to cut down the reed forest and clear the grass,' describing the action taken to address the mystery.
  • 既穷，得一穴，中有缯帛食器 - 'When they dug deeper, they found a hole with silk, cloth, and food containers,' revealing the cause of the disappearances.
  • 见野狸十余，有颦而俯者，呻而仰者，瞬而乳者，偃而踞者，嗷然若相愁状 - 'They saw more than ten wild cats, some frowning and bowing, some moaning and looking up, some blinking and nursing, some lying down and sitting, all making sounds as if they were in distress,' vividly describing the cats.
  • 民尽杀之，自是里民用安其居 - 'The residents killed all of them, and from then on, the residents lived in peace,' concluding the story with the resolution of the problem.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 岩既悟其妖异，心颇怪悸 - This sentence combines Ran's realization of the supernatural with his emotional response, requiring careful rendering to convey both the intellectual and emotional aspects.
  • 适遇有故人自濮上来，以一黑犬见惠 - The sentence structure is elliptical, and the relationship between the old acquaintance and the black dog must be clarified in English.
  • 其猿为犬所啮，因而遁去 - The passive construction and the cause-and-effect relationship must be preserved in English.
  • 竟不穷其事，因录以传之 - The contrast between not investigating further and recording the event must be maintained.
  • 岩后以明经入仕，终于秦州上邽尉 - The sequence of events in Ran's career must be clearly articulated.
  • 客有游于太原者，偶于铜锅店精舍解鞍憩焉 - The traveler's journey and his stop at the hermitage must be logically connected.
  • 得刘君所传之事，而文甚鄙，后亡其本 - The sequence of finding the story, noting its crudeness, and its eventual loss must be preserved.

  • The phrase '自邑中游焉' is elliptical and can be expanded to '自邑中出游焉' for clarity.
  • The request '幸吾子纸笔书其事' is formal and polite, but the exact structure can be clarified for modern English.
  • The sequence of actions (bowing, inviting to sit) in '揖韦坐田野' should be clearly separated in English.
  • The act of pouring wine and drinking in '注酒一饮韦' should be clearly described.
  • The phrase '引数犬' can be expanded to '引数犬而来' for clarity.
  • The transformation in '化为一狐' is sudden and should be clearly indicated.
  • The discovery of the skull in '乃一髑髅' should be clearly linked to the previous events.
  • The description of the wine in '酒若牛溺之状' should be clear and vivid.
  • The illness in '因病热' should be clearly linked to the previous events.
  • The recovery in '月余方瘳' should be clearly indicated as a prolonged process.

  • 邺郡人有好育鹰隼者 - There was someone from Yecheng who liked raising falcons and hawks. This phrase introduces the main subject of the story.
  • 其鹰甚神俊 - The falcon was extremely spirited and agile. This is a descriptive phrase that highlights the quality of the falcon.
  • 邺人家所育鹰隼极多，皆莫能比 - The family in Yecheng raised many falcons and hawks, none of which could compare to this one. This emphasizes the exceptional nature of the falcon.
  • 常臂以玩不去手 - He would often carry it on his arm for play, never letting it out of his hand. This describes the close relationship between the owner and the falcon.
  • 后有东夷人见者，请以缯百余段为直 - Later, an Eastern barbarian saw it and offered to pay more than a hundred pieces of silk for it. This introduces a potential buyer and the value of the falcon.
  • 吾方念此不知其所用 - I am just thinking about this and don't know what use it will have. This expresses the owner's uncertainty about the falcon's purpose.
  • 此海鹞也，善辟蛟螭患，君宜于邺城南放之，可以见其用矣 - This is a sea hawk, good at warding off the dangers of dragons and chimeras. You should release it in the south of Yecheng to see its use. This provides information about the falcon's special abilities and suggests a specific action.
  • 先是，邺城南陂蛟常为人患，郡民苦之有年矣 - Before this, the dragon in the southern pond of Yecheng had been a constant nuisance to the people of the city for many years. This sets up the background context for the problem that the falcon will solve.
  • 海鹞忽投陂水中，顷之乃出，得一小蛟 - The sea hawk suddenly dove into the pond, and after a while, it emerged with a small dragon in its beak. This describes the falcon's action and success.
  • 既出，食之且尽 - After emerging, it finished eating the dragon. This concludes the falcon's mission.
  • 自是邺民免其患 - From then on, the people of Yecheng were free from the dragon's troubles. This highlights the positive outcome of the falcon's actions.
  • 有告于嵩，乃命邺人讯其事，邺人遂以海鹞献焉 - Someone reported this to Xue Song, and he ordered the person from Yecheng to investigate the matter. The person from Yecheng then presented the sea hawk to Xue Song. This concludes the narrative with the official recognition of the falcon's value.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 常自省郎出為永州司马 - Standard bureaucratic phrase indicating a demotion or transfer from a central office (the *Shenglang*, or *Shenglange*) to a local position as *Shimashi* (prefectural administrator) in Yongzhou.
  • 途至荆门 - A phrase indicating a journey to *Jingmen*, a city in Hubei province, historically a strategic and cultural hub.
  • 舍驿亭中 - Classical expression meaning 'to stay in the驿站 (post station) pavilion,' indicating temporary lodging during travel.
  • 再拜而泣 - Standard expression of deep respect and emotion, involving two bows and weeping.
  • 某，家楚水者也 - Classical self-introduction: 'I am one who dwells by the *Chushui* (a river or region in ancient China).'
  • 非君不能活之 - Formal and dramatic statement: 'Only you can save me.'
  • 傥获其生，不独戴恩而已 - 'If you save my life, I will not only be grateful, but...' – a classical way of introducing a promise or reward.
  • 兼能假君禄，益君为将为相，且无难矣 - 'Also, I can grant you official rank, help you become a general or chancellor, and it will be no difficulty.' This is a supernatural promise, typical in classical Chinese ghost stories.
  • 公谢而许之 - Standard response: 'The official bowed and accepted.'
  • 既寤，嘿自异之 - 'After waking, he silently found it strange.' *嘿* means 'silently' or 'in silence.'
  • 及再寐，又梦妇人且祈且谢，久而方去 - 'When he fell asleep again, he dreamed of the woman who both prayed and thanked him, and only after a long time did she leave.' This reflects the classical narrative structure of repeated dreams and supernatural encounters.
  • 明晨，有吏来，称荆帅命，将宴宗元 - 'The next morning, an official came, claiming to be delivering an order from the *Jing Shuai* (commander of Jingzhou), to host a banquet for Zongyuan.'
  • 因假寐焉 - 'He pretended to sleep' or 'took a nap,' a common literary device to delay or avoid an obligation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 柳宗元 - *Liu Zongyuan* (773–819), a famous Tang dynasty writer, philosopher, and official, one of the *Eight Great Prose Masters of the Tang and Song Dynasties*. He was exiled to Yongzhou and later柳州 (Liuzhou) in Guangxi, hence the title *Liuzhou Shimashi* (Prefect of Liuzhou).
  • 唐柳州刺史河东柳宗元 - 'Liu Zongyuan, the Tang dynasty *Shizhushi* (prefect) of Liuzhou, from Hedong.' *Hedong* (Hezhong) refers to the area around modern-day Shanxi province, the ancestral home of the Liu family.
  • 荆门 - *Jingmen*, a city in present-day Hubei province, known in ancient times for its strategic location on the Yangtze River.
  • 荆帅 - *Jing Shuai*, the commander or military governor of Jingzhou, a region that included Jingmen.
  • 宴宗元 - To host a banquet for Liu Zongyuan, likely a political gesture or test of loyalty from the local commander.
  • 假寐 - To feign sleep, a common literary trope to avoid or delay an obligation, often used in classical Chinese stories involving supernatural or political intrigue.

Proper Nouns

  • 柳宗元 - *Liu Zongyuan*, a proper noun and historical figure. Should be kept in transliteration with consistent spelling.
  • 唐 - *Tang*, the dynasty. Should be rendered as 'the Tang dynasty' or 'the Tang.'
  • 柳州 - *Liuzhou*, a city in present-day Guangxi. Should be transliterated as 'Liuzhou.'
  • 河东 - *Hedong*, a region in modern-day Shanxi, the ancestral home of Liu Zongyuan. Should be transliterated as 'Hedong.'
  • 荆门 - *Jingmen*, a city in Hubei. Transliterated as 'Jingmen.'
  • 荆帅 - *Jing Shuai*, the military commander of Jingzhou. Transliterated as 'the commander of Jingzhou' or 'the Jingzhou commander.'
  • 永州 - *Yongzhou*, a city in present-day Hunan. Transliterated as 'Yongzhou.'
  • 司马 - *Shimashi*, a title for a local official. Should be translated as 'prefectural administrator' or 'prefect.'
  • 驿亭 - *Yi Ting*, a post station or驿站 pavilion. Transliterated as 'post station pavilion' or 'station pavilion.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宗元 - Refers to Liu Zongyuan (刘宗元), a prominent literary figure and one of the Eight Great Prose Masters of the Tang Dynasty, known for his essays and political involvement.

  • 投江 - 'To cast into the river' is a reference to a form of ritual or religious practice, possibly to atone for a wrong or to return a life to the water, reflecting traditional beliefs in the afterlife or the spirit world.
  • 亡其首 - 'Lacking a head' in the dream may symbolize death, loss of authority, or a warning of impending doom.

Proper Nouns

  • 由是肩舁至江夏 - Means 'as a result, he was carried to Jiangxia on a stretcher,' describing the method of transportation.
  • 医药无及，竟以左臂溃为血，血尽而终 - Means 'medicine could not help, and eventually, his left arm became sores filled with blood, and he died after the blood ran out,' describing the final outcome.
  • 先是，韦君先夫人在江夏 - Means 'before this, Wei's wife was in Jiangxia,' setting the stage for the dream.
  • 梦一白衣人谓曰 - Means 'dreamed of a white-clad person who said,' describing the dream.
  • 我弟兄三人，其二人为汝子所杀 - Means 'we three brothers, two of us were killed by your son,' revealing the identity of the spiders.
  • 吾告上帝，帝用悯其冤，且遂吾请 - Means 'I told the Heavenly Emperor, who pitied our injustice and granted my request,' explaining the divine intervention.
  • 言毕，夫人惊寤 - Means 'after saying this, the wife woke up in shock,' describing the end of the dream.
  • 甚异之，恶不能言 - Means 'she found it very strange and could not express her feelings,' describing her emotional state.
  • 后旬余而韦君至，具得其状，方寤所梦 - Means 'a few weeks later, Wei arrived, and she learned the details, realizing what she had dreamed,' connecting the dream to reality.
  • 觉为梦日，果其馆亭时也 - Means 'realizing that the day of the dream was the same as the day he stayed at the inn,' confirming the connection.
  • 夫人泣曰：“其能久乎？” - Means 'the wife wept and said, 'How long can he last?'' expressing her concern.
  • 数日而韦君终矣 - Means 'a few days later, Wei died,' concluding the story.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 御史 - A high-ranking official in ancient China, responsible for supervising and reporting on the conduct of other officials.
  • 江夏 - A region in ancient China, located in present-day Hubei Province.
  • 商于 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location along the route.
  • 馆亭 - A type of inn or lodging place.
  • 上帝 - Refers to the Heavenly Emperor, a deity in Chinese mythology.
  • 先夫人 - Refers to the wife of the deceased, emphasizing her status and relationship to the deceased.

Proper Nouns

  • 韦君 - The name of the magistrate.
  • 江夏 - A region in ancient China.
  • 商于 - A place name.
  • 馆亭 - A type of inn.
  • 御史 - A high-ranking official.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 后数月 - Indicates a passage of time.
  • 有胡人数辈挈酒食诣其门 - A group of several barbarians brought wine and food to his door.
  • 既坐，顾谓颙曰 - After sitting down, they turned and said to Yu.
  • 吾南越人，长蛮貊中 - I am a person from Nanyue, growing up among the barbarians.
  • 闻唐天子庠罗天下英俊 - I have heard that the Emperor of Tang gathers the talented people from all over the world.
  • 且欲以文物化动四夷 - And wishes to use culture and civilization to influence the four barbarian tribes.
  • 故我航海梯山来中华，将观太学文物之光 - Therefore, I have sailed across the sea and climbed over the mountains to come to China, to see the brilliance of the Taixue's culture and civilization.
  • 唯吾子峨焉其冠，襜焉其裾，庄然其容，肃然其仪 - Only your son has a towering hat, a flowing robe, a dignified appearance, and a solemn demeanor.
  • 真唐朝儒生也 - Truly, he is a scholar of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 故我愿与子交欢 - Therefore, I wish to befriend you.
  • 颙谢曰 - Yu thanked them, saying.
  • 颙幸得籍于太学，然无他才能，何足下见爱之深也 - I am fortunate to be registered at the Taixue, but I have no other talents. Why do you love me so much?
  • 于是相与酣宴，极欢而去 - They then drank together, had a great feast, and left happily.
  • 颙，信士也，以为群胡不我欺 - Yu, a trustworthy man, believed that the group of barbarians did not deceive him.
  • 旬余，群胡又至，持金缯为颙寿 - After several days, the group of barbarians came again, bringing gold and silk to wish Yu a long life.
  • 颙至疑其有他，即固拒之 - Yu suspected that they had another intention, so he firmly refused them.
  • 胡人曰 - The barbarians said.
  • 吾子居长安中，惶惶然有饥寒色，故持金缯为子仆马一日之费，所以交吾子欢耳 - Your son lives in Chang'an, looking hungry and cold. Therefore, we brought gold and silk to cover your expenses for a day, so that we could be friends with you.
  • 岂有他哉幸勿疑我也 - We have no other intentions. Please do not doubt us.
  • 颙不得已，受金缯 - Yu had no choice but to accept the gold and silk.
  • 及胡人去，太学中诸生闻之，偕来谓颙曰 - When the barbarians left, the other students at the Taixue heard about it and came together to tell Yu.
  • 彼胡率爱利不顾其身，争盐米之微，尚致相贼杀者，宁肯弃金缯为朋友寿乎 - Those barbarians are greedy for profit and do not care about themselves. They fight over small things like salt and rice, even to the point of killing each other. How could they give up gold and silk to wish a friend a long life?
  • 且太学中诸生甚多，何为烛厚君耶 - Moreover, there are many students at the Taixue. Why did you treat me so favorably?
  • 君匿身郊野间，以避再来也 - You hid yourself in the suburbs to avoid them coming again.
  • 遂侨居于渭水上，杜门不出 - He then moved to live by the Wei River and closed his door, not going out.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 胡人 - Refers to people of non-Han Chinese ethnicity, often from Central Asia, who were sometimes seen as barbarians.
  • 南越 - Refers to the region south of the Yangtze River, which was historically a frontier area.
  • 蛮貊 - A general term for non-Han Chinese peoples, often used to describe the regions surrounding China.
  • 唐天子 - Refers to the Emperor of the Tang Dynasty, a period known for its cultural and political achievements.
  • 太学 - The highest institution of learning in ancient China, where scholars and officials were trained.
  • 长安 - The capital city of the Tang Dynasty, now known as Xi'an.
  • 渭水 - A river in Shaanxi Province, where Yu eventually moved to live.
  • 诸生 - Refers to students or scholars at the Taixue.
  • 盐米 - Refers to basic necessities like salt and rice, often used to symbolize poverty.
  • 贼杀 - Refers to killing or murder, often used to describe conflicts over resources.
  • 杜门 - Refers to closing the door, symbolizing isolation or withdrawal from society.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 僅月余 - Means 'only a month or so,' indicating a short period of time.
  • 群胡又诣其门 - '群胡' refers to a group of people, possibly barbarians or foreigners; '诣其门' means 'came to his door.'
  • 比君在太学中，我未得尽言 - '比' means 'when' or 'during'; '太学' refers to the national university or academy.
  • 今君退居郊野，果吾心也 - '果吾心' means 'as I hoped' or 'as I wished.'
  • 且我所祈，于君固无害，于我则大惠也 - '且' means 'furthermore'; '所祈' means 'what I wish for'; '固无害' means 'certainly no harm'; '大惠' means 'great benefit.'
  • 谨受教 - A polite way of saying 'I will follow your advice.'
  • 好食面乎 - '好食面' means 'do you like eating noodles?'
  • 若诚之，又安有不可耶 - '若诚之' means 'if you truly believe'; '安有不可耶' means 'how can there be anything wrong?'
  • 盖有求于君耳 - '盖' means 'it is because'; '有求于君' means 'I have a request for you.'
  • 消面虫 - '消面虫' literally means 'noodle-eating worm.'
  • 天下之奇宝 - '天下之奇宝' means 'a rare treasure in the world.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太学 - Refers to the national university or academy, a prestigious institution for higher learning in ancient China.
  • 胡人 - Refers to people of non-Han Chinese ethnicity, often associated with the northern nomadic tribes.
  • 郊野 - Refers to the outskirts or countryside, suggesting a retreat from urban life.
  • 消面虫 - A fictional or mythical creature, possibly a metaphor for something unusual or valuable.
  • 天下之奇宝 - Refers to a rare or valuable item, often used to describe something extraordinary.

Proper Nouns

  • 郊野 - The outskirts or countryside.
  • 消面虫 - A fictional or mythical creature.
  • 天下之奇宝 - A rare or valuable item.

Idiomatic Expressions

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 僅歲余 - Means 'after only a year or so,' indicating a short period of time.
  • 吾子能與我偕游海中乎 - A rhetorical question meaning 'Can you, my son, accompany me on a journey to the sea?'
  • 而吾子岂非好奇之士耶 - A rhetorical question meaning 'And aren't you, my son, a man of curiosity?'
  • 颙既以甚富 - Means 'Yong, having become very wealthy.'
  • 又素享闲逸自遂 - Means 'and has always enjoyed a life of leisure and self-satisfaction.'

  • 胡人结宇而居 - Means 'the barbarians built a dwelling and settled there.'
  • 置油膏于银鼎中 - Means 'they placed oil and fat in a silver cauldron.'
  • 构火其下 - Means 'and set fire beneath it.'
  • 投虫于鼎中 - Means 'they threw insects into the cauldron.'
  • 炼之，七日不绝燎 - Means 'they refined them, the fire burning continuously for seven days.'
  • 忽有一童，分发，衣青襦 - Means 'suddenly, a child appeared, with disheveled hair and wearing a green tunic.'
  • 自海水中出 - Means 'emerging from the seawater.'
  • 捧月盘 - Means 'holding a moon-shaped tray.'
  • 盘中有径寸珠甚多 - Means 'the tray contained many pearls, each about an inch in diameter.'
  • 来献胡人 - Means 'coming to offer the pearls to the barbarians.'
  • 胡人大声叱之 - Means 'the barbarians shouted at him angrily.'
  • 其童色惧，捧盘而去 - Means 'the child, frightened, took the tray and left.'
  • 僮去食顷 - Means 'after the child had left for a meal's worth of time.'
  • 又有一玉女，貌极冶 - Means 'another jade maiden appeared, extremely beautiful.'
  • 衣雾绡之衣 - Means 'wearing a robe made of misty gauze.'
  • 佩王珥珠 - Means 'adorned with a royal diadem and pearl earrings.'
  • 翩翩自海中而出 - Means 'gracefully emerging from the sea.'
  • 捧紫玉盘，中有珠数十 - Means 'holding a purple jade tray containing dozens of pearls.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 群胡 - Refers to non-Han Chinese peoples, often used in a derogatory sense.

  • 胡人结宇而居 - The practice of building temporary dwellings is common in nomadic or semi-nomadic cultures.
  • 置油膏于银鼎中 - This ritualistic act may be related to alchemical or magical practices, often found in Daoist texts.
  • 投虫于鼎中，炼之 - This could be a reference to alchemical processes or magical rituals involving the transformation of substances.
  • 胡人 - The term '胡人' often carries negative connotations in Chinese literature, reflecting historical tensions between Han Chinese and non-Han peoples.

Proper Nouns
